title: aster puniceus var. elliottii
intro: Aster puniceus var. elliottii, also known as the Elliott's purple-stemmed aster, is a perennial flowering plant in the sunflower family native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Aster puniceus var. elliottii can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
